summary: Iter 40 — 5th retest of CapitalDetailModal bulk-delete modal-stays-open bug. Main agent applied BOTH iter 39 PRIMARY recommendations: (a) onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} + onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} on DialogContent, (b) removed disabled attribute from bulk-delete-btn (replaced with aria-disabled + onClick early return). VERIFIED via grep that the changes are in place at lines 145-150 and 287-301 of CapitalDetailModal.jsx. Result: BULK DELETE STILL CLOSES THE MODAL. Additionally surfaced two new findings: (1) single-delete trash icon (selection mode OFF) ALSO closes the modal; (2) onInteractOutside preventDefault has introduced a REGRESSION — outside-click no longer closes the modal, which contradicts the user's stated requirement.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal STILL closes after bulk delete despite onInteractOutside/onFocusOutside preventDefault and removal of disabled attribute. URL unchanged, so no reload — modal-stays-open part still FAILING (5th iter). After clicking Sil(9) on 9 movements, total-capital-amount becomes invisible and dashboard reappears. Hypothesis: reload() in handleBulkDelete calls setLoading(true) which unmounts the entire toolbar (CashTab early-returns 'Yükleniyor...') including the focused bulk-delete-btn. The unmount causes focus to leave the dialog scope. While onFocusOutside should catch this, Radix may be triggering onOpenChange via a different code path (e.g., FocusScope internal guard, or React StrictMode double-invoke).

Single-delete legacy trash icon (selection mode OFF) ALSO closes the modal. Same root cause hypothesized: setMovements removes the row → trash button unmounted → focus loss → modal closes. Probably the same fix that resolves bulk delete will resolve this.

REGRESSION: outside-click on backdrop no longer closes the modal. User explicitly required: 'Modal hâlâ kullanıcı X butonu tıklayınca veya Esc'e basınca veya dışarı tıklayınca kapanıyor'. Since onInteractOutside is the parent of onPointerDownOutside, preventDefault here blocks outside-click closes. Suggest: use only onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} (which is what the iter 39 RCA actually recommended) — DO NOT prevent onInteractOutside.

passed_tests: API seed via /api/capital/adjust deposit (5 movements seeded twice, 9 movements present in second run)
Tab switching (capital-tab-cash ↔ capital-tab-inventory) — modal stays open ✅
Bulk delete URL unchanged (no page reload) — primary no-reload bug remains FIXED for 5th iter
failed_tests: BULK DELETE — modal closes (5TH STRAIGHT FAIL). Pre: total ₺1.253.000, 9 movements, Sil(9) clicked. Post: total-capital-amount NOT visible, dashboard restored, URL unchanged. Screenshot: /app/.screenshots/iter40_bulk_fail.png
SINGLE DELETE (legacy trash icon, selection mode OFF) — modal closes after first row deleted. New finding this iter.
OUTSIDE CLICK — modal does NOT close (regression introduced by onInteractOutside preventDefault). User requires this behavior to remain.
action_items: ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATION NEEDED: even with onInteractOutside+onFocusOutside preventDefault AND no disabled-attribute on the focused button, the modal still closes after bulk delete. This means the close trigger is NOT one of (a) outside-pointer-down (b) focus-outside (c) Esc. Likely candidates to investigate: (i) reload() in handleBulkDelete sets setLoading(true) → CashTab early-returns 'Yükleniyor...' → bulk-delete-btn unmounted → focus to <body>. Even though Radix FocusScope should refocus inside the dialog, something is dispatching onOpenChange(false). Try logging: <Dialog onOpenChange={(open) => { console.log('onOpenChange', open, new Error().stack); if (!open) onClose(); }}> to capture the call site/stack. (ii) fetchData() / refreshCapital() may be causing Dashboard to re-mount or capitalDetailOpen to reset (unlikely but check React DevTools / add a useEffect log). (iii) React StrictMode double-effect may interact with Radix internals.

FIX REGRESSION: Remove onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()}. Keep ONLY onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()}. This restores outside-click closing while still guarding against focus-loss-induced auto-close. (iter 39 specifically recommended onFocusOutside; onInteractOutside was not advised because it is the union and over-broad.)

PROPOSED ALTERNATIVE FIX (defense-in-depth): change handleBulkDelete to NOT call reload() — only do the optimistic setMovements filter. Skip refreshCapital/fetchData here (capital_movements deletion already adjusts capital server-side; ensure the server-mutated capital is reflected via the next legitimate refresh, e.g., on next modal open). This avoids the loading=true → unmount cascade. Same applies to handleDeleteMovement.

PROPOSED ALTERNATIVE FIX (cheapest): keep the toolbar (Seç/Tümünü Seç/Sil/İptal buttons) RENDERED even when loading=true. Currently CashTab returns the 'Yükleniyor...' div early and discards the toolbar. Render the toolbar always; show 'Yükleniyor...' only inside the list area. This keeps bulk-delete-btn mounted and focused.

RECOMMENDED COMBO: (a) drop onInteractOutside preventDefault (fix regression), (b) keep toolbar mounted during loading (fix focus-loss), (c) keep the optimistic setMovements + skip reload() during bulk-delete to remove the unmount cascade entirely.
critical_code_review_comments: After 5 iterations the bug remains unresolved. The recurring pattern: each fix addresses ONE focus-loss vector but a different one surfaces. The structural problem is that handleBulkDelete and handleDeleteMovement both trigger reload() which unmounts the focused button via the loading=true early return in CashTab. Fix the unmount cascade at its root by either skipping reload() (rely on optimistic + next natural refresh) or by NOT unmounting the toolbar during loading.
onInteractOutside is over-broad — it suppresses pointer-down-outside which is a legitimate close vector. Use onFocusOutside only.

CashTab's early return for `loading` is what causes the toolbar/buttons to disappear during reload(). Change to a non-unmounting loading indicator, e.g., render the toolbar always and overlay/replace just the list rows.
Triple state refresh (reload + refreshCapital + fetchData) at lines 94-96 is still redundant and likely the root of the focus-loss cascade.
Comment at lines 90-91 ('bulk-delete-btn DOM'da ve focus'lu kalır') is still incorrect — when reload() flips loading=true, the button DOES leave the DOM.

success_rate.frontend: 55% — primary modal-stays-open bug still failing for 5th iter; new bug (single-delete) and new regression (outside-click no longer closes) introduced by latest fix

seed_data_creation: Seeded 9 cash movements via /api/capital/adjust deposit (4+5). All 9 deleted by the bulk-delete test. After this iter, cash dropped back to baseline ~₺145.000. Re-seed via the same API for next iter.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After 5 iterations the modal-closes-on-delete bug is unresolved. CURRENT RCA (newly refined this iter): handleBulkDelete (and handleDeleteMovement) call reload() → setLoading(true) → CashTab early-returns 'Yükleniyor...' div, UNMOUNTING the toolbar including the focused bulk-delete-btn (or trash button). Focus jumps to <body>. Even though onFocusOutside preventDefault is applied, modal still closes — investigate Radix internal close path via console logging in <Dialog onOpenChange> and React DevTools. Recommended fix combo: (1) onFocusOutside only (drop onInteractOutside preventDefault to restore outside-click closing), (2) keep the toolbar always mounted (do not early-return on loading), (3) remove the reload() call from handleBulkDelete/handleDeleteMovement (rely on optimistic + next refresh). Re-seed via: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST $URL/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"test_branch_6b287ede@test.com","password":"Password123!"}' | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print(d.get("token") or d.get("access_token",""))') ; for i in 1..5; do curl -X POST $URL/api/capital/adjust -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"type":"deposit","amount":1000,"description":"TEST"}'; done. Test files: this run, no new test files. Iter 39 report: /app/test_reports/iteration_39.json.
rca_of_the_issue: After 5 iterations and applying every recommended fix (remove blur(), remove disabled attribute, add onFocusOutside preventDefault, add onInteractOutside preventDefault), the modal STILL closes on bulk delete and now also on single-delete. The new RCA hypothesis: handleBulkDelete/handleDeleteMovement call reload() which sets loading=true. CashTab does an early-return rendering only 'Yükleniyor...' when loading=true (line 245-247). This unmounts the entire toolbar — including the focused bulk-delete-btn or trash button. The browser moves focus to <body>. Although onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} is set on DialogContent, Radix may still dispatch onOpenChange(false) via a separate code path (e.g., FocusScope's internal handling of orphaned focus, or because the focus-out happens during a React commit phase that bypasses the event handler chain). The cleanest structural fix is to either (a) NOT unmount the toolbar on loading (render it always; show loading only on the list portion), (b) skip the reload() entirely (rely on optimistic update + next natural refresh), or (c) BOTH. Additionally, onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} is over-broad and broke outside-click closing — replace with onFocusOutside-only. Reproduction: login → click [data-testid='capital-amount-display'] → if movements >0, either click [data-testid^='delete-movement-'].first OR enter selection mode → toggle-select-all → bulk-delete → wait 3s → assert is_visible('[data-testid="total-capital-amount"]') === True (currently False).
